### Post by rav0r on 2009-06-21
When I drag my mouse down, the screen goes up, leaving a grey band under.
It's annoying and I couldn't find the problem in xorg.conf.

```

# nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
# nvidia-settings:  version 1.0  (buildd@crested)  Mon Nov  3 08:46:04 UTC 2008

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "CRT-0"
    HorizSync       30.0 - 130.0
    VertRefresh     48.0 - 170.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Device0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    Option         "TwinView" "1"
    Option         "TwinViewXineramaInfoOrder" "CRT-0"
    Option         "metamodes" "CRT-0: 1280x960 +0+0, CRT-1: 1280x960 +1280+0; CRT-0: 1152x864 +0+0, CRT-1: nvidia-auto-select +1152+0; CRT-0: 1024x768 +0+0, CRT-1: nvidia-auto-select +1024+0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth       24
    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "dbe"
    Load           "extmod"
    Load           "type1"
    Load           "freetype"
    Load           "glx"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Mouse0"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Keyboard0"
    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Device0"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "GeForce 8800 GTX"
    Driver    "nvidia"
    Option    "NoLogo"    "True"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
    Option         "Xinerama" "0"
EndSection

```

---

### Post by xc3RnbFO8P on 2009-06-21
It could be that your monitor doesn't support 1280x960
or you could check 
System> Preferences> Display and see supported resolution there

---

### Post by rav0r on 2009-06-22
Of course it supports, i've got two identical monitors, it has nothing to do with that.
PS: Solved, it was set to 1280x1024 instead of 960 from system settings.

---

### Post by rav0r on 2009-07-28
Actually, not really solved, each time I log in, I have to go to system settings-> display, else the trouble remains.
